King George III was sitting on the throne in England when the American colonies declared independence in 1776.
George Washington served 2 terms as president of the United States.
John Adams served as George Washington's vice president.
Francis Scott Key wrote 'The Star-Spangled Banner.' He penned the lyrics in 1814 after witnessing the British bombardment of Fort McHenry during the War of 1812.
The distinguishing feature of the Liberty Bell is its famous crack (fracture), which made the bell iconic and eventually rendered it unable to be rung.
John Adams and Thomas Jefferson both died on July 4, 1826.
E pluribus unum means 'Out of many, one.' It signifies that out of many states or individuals, a single unified nation is formed.
The American bison (North American bison) is the national mammal of the United States.
A score is 20 years. Thus, 'four score and seven years ago' refers to 87 years before 1863, pointing back to 1776 and the founding of the nation.
The 19th Amendment to the US Constitution granted American women the right to vote.
The Stock Market Crash of 1929 (also known as Black Tuesday, October 29, 1929) triggered the Great Depression in the US.
Alan Shepard was the first American in space, making a suborbital flight on May 5, 1961.
President Joe Biden signed the bill making Juneteenth a federal holiday commemorating the end of slavery in the US.
